_meta.experiment: Official IFEval re-measurement (validate Paper B custom-checker instruction-following claim)
_meta.harness: lm-eval-harness official 'ifeval' task, limit=100, sasori joint-ridge fake-quant g=256

K3.note: >FP = noise: FP baseline depressed by thinking-mode artifact

verdict_vs_paperB: CONFIRMS (no correction needed). Paper B (custom checkers) claimed instruction-following survives K2 at ~64-86%. Official lm-eval IFEval: Qwen2.5-7B K2 instruction-level retention 69-70%, Qwen3-8B K2 instruction-level 84-88% -> both fall within/around the 64-86% band => Paper B's IFEval claim is VALIDATED by the official metric. Nuances (do not overclaim): (a) PROMPT-level retention is lower (~53-68%) because it is a conjunctive all-instructions-in-prompt metric, harder than instruction-level; (b) K3 is near-lossless for instruction-following (Qwen2.5-7B 92-99%); (c) Qwen3-8B FP baseline is depressed (inst-strict 0.368) by a thinking-mode artifact (<think> blocks break the format checker), so its retention ratios are noisy and its K3>FP is noise at low absolutes -> treat Qwen3-8B as caveated. ACTION: no Paper B edit required; the claim stands. Optional enhancement: Paper B could cite this official confirmation of its custom-checker IFEval.
